name: Suwar ibn Munim
title: Wounded Captive
shortDescription: A brave companion who was severely wounded and captured in battle, and martyred six months later due to his injuries.
fullStory:
Sawwar ibn Mun'im al-Himyari (or al-Hamdani) was a sincere Shia and a brave warrior in Imam Hussain's (AS) army.

On Ashura, Sawwar fought with all his might and, after a long battle inflicting heavy casualties on the enemy, his body sustained numerous wounds. Due to severe bleeding and injuries, he lost consciousness on the battlefield and was captured.

Umar ibn Sa'd intended to kill him, but since Sawwar belonged to a powerful tribe, his tribesmen interceded for him, and he was transferred to Kufa and imprisoned (or kept under guard).

Sawwar endured the pain of his Karbala wounds for six whole months until he finally joined the caravan of Karbala martyrs due to those same injuries. He is a symbol of resistance and the oppression of wounded captives.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلَى الجَرِيحِ المَأْسُورِ، سَوّارِ بْنِ أَبِي حِمْيَرٍ...
quote: (Ziyarat al-Nahiya:) Peace be upon the wounded captive, Sawwar ibn Abi Himyar...
